# nxos_nxapi (Resource)

This resource can manage the NX-API configuration on NX-OS devices, including VRF, HTTP/HTTPS ports, SSL settings, certificate configuration, and client certificate authentication.

### API Documentation

- [nxapiInst](https://pubhub.devnetcloud.com/media/dme-docs-10-5-3/docs/System/nxapi:Inst/)

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "nxos_nxapi" "example" {
  vrf                               = "management"
  http_port                         = 80
  https_port                        = 443
  idle_timeout                      = 10
  certificate_enable                = false
  certificate_file                  = "bootflash:server.crt"
  key_file                          = "bootflash:server.key"
  encrypted_key_passphrase          = "mypassphrase"
  trustpoint                        = "mytrustpoint"
  ssl_protocols                     = "TLSv1.2"
  ssl_ciphers_weak                  = false
  client_certificate_authentication = "optional"
  sudi                              = false
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `certificate_enable` (Boolean) Certificate status of NX-API.
- `certificate_file` (String) Certificate file for NX-API.
- `client_certificate_authentication` (String) Enable Mutual SSL Certificate Authentication for NXAPI between client and server and set verficiation restriction level.
  - Choices: `off`, `optional`, `strict`, `two-step`
- `device` (String) A device name from the provider configuration.
- `encrypted_key_passphrase` (String, Sensitive) Passphrase to decrypt the encrypted key file for NX-API.
- `encrypted_key_passphrase_wo` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The write-only value of the attribute.
- `encrypted_key_passphrase_wo_version` (Number) The write-only version of the attribute.
- `http_port` (Number) HTTP Port.
  - Range: `-1`-`65535`
- `https_port` (Number) HTTPS Port.
  - Range: `-1`-`65535`
- `idle_timeout` (Number) Specifies the idle timeout (in minutes) before a session expires.
  - Range: `1`-`1440`
- `key_file` (String) Key file for NX-API.
- `ssl_ciphers_weak` (Boolean) Enable Weak Ciphers for SSL.
- `ssl_protocols` (String) String of space separated SSL protocols to be supported.
- `sudi` (Boolean) SUDI support for nginx.
- `trustpoint` (String) Trustpoint label for NX-API.
- `vrf` (String) Vrf for NX-API.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The distinguished name of the object.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to       = nxos_nxapi.example
  identity = {}
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema


#### Optional

- `device` (String) A device name from the provider configuration.

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = nxos_nxapi.example
  id = ""
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import nxos_nxapi.example ""
```
